prose:
Method–provenance summary (all fields explicit)

• Property computed: HOMO–LUMO gap (difference between the α-spin frontier orbital energies) in electron-volts (eV).
• Electronic structure level: Density-functional theory, B3LYP functional with the def2-TZVP Gaussian basis set (tier ‘standard’), using exact four-center integrals (no RI density fitting).
• Geometry: fixed input coordinates from /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/frontier-orbitals-validation/furan/furan.xyz (no further optimisation; single-point electronic structure calculation, so result is DFT//input-XYZ).
• Environment: gas phase (no implicit solvent).
• Charge = 0, multiplicity = 1 (closed-shell singlet).
• Software backend: PySCF  (invoked via chemkit ‘frontier-orbitals’ skill; full command recorded in `cli_invocation`).
• SCF convergence: converged in 9 iterations; integrity.status = ok, trustworthy = true.
• Engine warnings: none reported (`warnings` array empty → “no warnings reported”).
• Live run log was streamed during execution to a `frontier_<timestamp>.out` file (path printed at launch) and the full JSON result is saved at the location shown in `cli_invocation`.

Headline result (B3LYP/def2-TZVP, gas phase, 0/1):
  HOMO energy  =  –6.3597638 eV
  LUMO energy  =  –0.1024036 eV
  HOMO–LUMO gap = **6.2573602 eV**

No experimental comparison requested; value is purely computed at the stated level of theory.
